title: ISE RADIUS Change-of-Authorization (CoA) Failure Detection

compliance.controlObjective: Incident-response closure evidence for change-of-authorization workflows.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Misconfigure CoA shared secret on a lab NAD. Apply ANC quarantine to an endpoint on that NAD. ISE emits 5419 (CoA failed) and the NAD emits %RADIUS-3-COA_NACK. UC-17.1.56 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fix the shared secret. Quarantine succeeds via 5417/5418. Alert clears.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Authentication* category — CoA codes `5417` (CoA initiated), `5418` (CoA succeeded), `5419` (CoA failed), `5420` (CoA timeout), `5421` (CoA NAS not responding). Fields: `coa_action` (port-bounce|port-shutdown|reauth|disconnect), `NAS-IP-Address`, `endpoint_mac`, `error_code`. NAD-side: `index=network sourcetype=cisco:ios` `%RADIUS-3-COA_NACK`. CIM mapping: `Change.action`.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` ([Splunkbase 1915](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1915)) + Cisco network TA.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5417, 5419, 5420, 5421))
  OR (index=network sourcetype="cisco:ios" "%RADIUS-3-COA")
| eval action = case(MessageCode=="5417", "initiated", MessageCode=="5419", "failed", MessageCode=="5420", "timeout", MessageCode=="5421", "nas_no_response", like(_raw, "%COA_NACK%"), "nas_nack")
| stats count by NAS-IP-Address, action, coa_action
| where action != "initiated" AND count > 5
description: Detects RADIUS CoA failures, timeouts, and NAS no-response events at scale. CoA is how ISE pushes runtime authorization changes (quarantine, reauth, port-bounce) to NADs — failures mean the enforcement action didn't reach the device.
value: When SOAR pushes a quarantine to ISE and ISE pushes a CoA to the NAD that fails, the endpoint *remains uncontained*. CoA failure detection is the closure validation for incident-response automation.
implementation: Confirm *Authentication* category at INFO. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, throttle suppression on `NAS-IP-Address` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: CoA failures last 1h. (2) Top-N NADs by failure count. (3) Failure-class breakdown.

references.title: RFC 5176 — Dynamic Authorization Extensions to RADIUS

knownFalsePositives: (a) **NAD reload** — During NAD reload, CoA pkts time out; suppress within scheduled change window. (b) **CoA shared-secret mismatch** — One-time misconfiguration; permanent failure on that NAD. Triage as config issue, not transient. (c) **Older NAD without CoA support** — Some legacy gear doesn't support CoA; tag and exclude.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• NAD configured with `aaa server radius dynamic-author` (and matching shared secret).
• `nad_inventory.csv` with `nas_ip`, `coa_supported`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — CoA Health":
• Single value: failures last 1h.
• Top-N NADs.
• Failure-class.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on sustained CoA failure for one NAD.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Shared-secret mismatch** — Realign on NAD and ISE.
• **NAS no response** — UDP/3799 (or 1700) blocked.
• **CoA initiated but NAD doesn't enforce** — NAD has `aaa authorization network ... default group ...` not configured to honour CoA.
grandmaExplanation: When ISE tells a switch 'kick this person off the network' via a special message, we make sure the switch actually heard the message and acted on it.
